meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template Tutorial
content.seo_description: Master the Antigravity project template to overcome design limitations. A high-speed, intermediate guide for freelancers using Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template bridges the gap between functional code and professional UI by utilizing standardized, component-driven architecture. By pairing this template with Claude Code, you automate the heavy lifting of design, allowing you to generate production-ready SaaS interfaces instantly without needing advanced design skills or external UI libraries.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat. Your client expects a high-fidelity SaaS dashboard by Friday, but your design skills are purely functional—you build logic, not aesthetics. The friction between your technical competence and the visual expectations of the market is the silent killer of freelance profit margins.

Stop fighting the CSS cascade and start leveraging structural intelligence. By integrating the Antigravity project template with Claude Code, you transform from a solitary developer into a high-velocity product studio. This isn't just about writing code; it’s about deploying a refined, design-system-backed architecture that makes every project look like it was built by a million-dollar agency.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Framework
steps.description: Establish the foundational structure that enforces design consistency before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the standard Antigravity template, ensuring the design system uses a modern, high-contrast dark mode theme with Tailwind CSS utility classes.

steps.title: Define Component Architecture
steps.description: Map your specific feature requirements to the template's existing component library to ensure visual uniformity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my requirements for a dashboard with user analytics and settings; map these to the Antigravity component library, prioritizing the 'Card' and 'DataGrid' primitives.

steps.title: Inject Business Logic via Claude Code
steps.description: Use AI to bridge the gap between your API endpoints and the pre-built UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Connect the 'DataGrid' component to my backend API. Ensure the data fetching follows the Antigravity pattern for loading states and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Integrate API endpoints into existing UI components using the standard Antigravity data-fetching pattern."

steps.title: Refine Responsive Breakpoints
steps.description: Automatically adjust the layout for mobile devices using the template's built-in responsiveness engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current layout for mobile responsiveness. Apply Antigravity standard breakpoint overrides to ensure the dashboard remains usable on mobile viewports.

steps.title: Final Polish and Build
steps.description: Run the final build pipeline to optimize assets and enforce strict linting rules provided by the template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the final build command and ensure all Antigravity design tokens are correctly compiled into the CSS output.

pro_tips: Always define your design tokens in the Antigravity config file before generating components to ensure global visual consistency.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your client's specific brand colors so they are applied automatically to every new component generated.
When facing a design bug, ask the AI to 'revert to the Antigravity base class' to instantly strip away conflicting user-added CSS.
Structure your project documentation inside the Antigravity metadata field; this allows Claude Code to 'understand' the context of your app during complex refactors.
Leverage the template's built-in 'Skeleton' components during initial development to maintain a professional look even before the backend data is ready.
content.next_steps: After completing your build, deploy to a staging environment using Vercel or Netlify, then use the Claude Code 'review' command to identify any accessibility gaps before the client preview.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations slow down your freelance revenue. By mastering the Antigravity workflow, you reclaim hours of development time and deliver world-class interfaces every time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
